text: 1. The formation of $\mathrm { F e } _ { 3 } \mathrm { A l }$ ordered phase has been observed in binary Fe-Al based low- density steels. These phases have been characterized by SAXS, Mössbauer spectroscopy and TEM which shows that size of the ordered phase to be between 2.5–3.8 nm. The size of the ordered phase is observed to be increasing with the aluminium content.

2. The tensile yield strengths of the Fe-6.8Al low-density steel samples subjected to different annealing conditions were modelled with the available theoretical formulations for various strengthening mechanisms. For the cold rolled sample, strengthening contributions from (i) dislocations (generated during cold rolling) and (ii) grain size/boundaries were observed to be the dominant. For the fully recrystallised samples, the contribution to the strengthening mechanisms was in the following order: (i) grain size strengthening (dominant mechanism), followed by nearly equal contribution from (ii) dislocations and (iii) ordered $\mathrm { F e } _ { 3 } \mathrm { A l }$ phase. The material annealed even beyond complete recrystallization underwent higher extent of ordering, and hence overall strength was due to nearly equal contributions from (i) solid solution strengthening and (ii) order strengthening. In summary, it can be stated that the dominance and interplay of various strengthening mechanisms including order strengthening leads to the enhanced strengthening in Fe-Al alloys. # Acknowledgements The authors are grateful to Prof. R. K. Ray for motivating us to carry out this work. We deeply acknowledge Mr. S. Sashidhara, Indian Institute of Science and Mr. M. Ramakrishna, ARCI for their help in carrying out the tension tests and electron microscopy sample preparation. Mr. Rajib Kalsar at department of Materials Engineering, IISc, Bangalore is acknowledged for the TEM sample preparation and doing TEM experiments. The authors also acknowledge the use of microscopic facilities of IISc, Bangalore.
